title: arctium × nothum subsp. debrayi
intro: Arctium × nothum subsp. debrayi, also known as Burdock, is a biennial herb native to Europe, North Africa, and Western Asia, belonging to the dandelion and thistle family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. Burdock root has been found to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function, as well as being used to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Burdock can be consumed as a tea, taken as a supplement, or used in herbal preparations like infusions, tinctures, and salves, making it a versatile and accessible remedy for various health issues.
